name: Prados del Rey Loop from Baza
name_original: Los Prados del Rey desde Baza — circular por Parque Natural Sierra de Baza
description: This difficult 34.2-mile mountain biking route in Sierra de Baza Natural Park offers challenging terrain and stunning high-mountain meadows.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Baza offers access to an extensive network of trails within the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. This region is characterized by diverse landscapes, including pine forests, ancient poplars, and varied terrain. The geological formations contribute to a unique environment for cycling. An extensive trail network caters to mountain bikers of various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Baza The most popular mountain bike trail is Visitor center Cortijo Narváez from Baza — circular through the Natural Park Sierra de Baza, a 19.7 miles (31.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 59 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers varied terrain through the natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Eye of the Heredad - circular by Zujar from Baza, a moderate 25.9 miles (41.7 km) path. This route provides scenic views and diverse landscapes around Zújar. Local mountain bikers also love the Los Prados del Rey desde Baza — circular por Parque Natural Sierra de Baza, a 34.2 miles (55.0 km) trail leading through the Sierra de Baza Natural Park, often completed in about 5 hours 10 minutes. Mountain biking around Baza is defined by the expansive Sierra de Baza Natural Park, diverse forested areas, and varied terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more difficult climbs. The routes in Baza are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 90 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Baza's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Baza?
A: There are over 90 mountain bike trails available around Baza, primarily within the expansive Sierra de Baza Natural Park. These routes cater to a wide range of skill levels, from easy paths to more challenging climbs.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Baza?
A: The mountain bike trails around Baza offer varied difficulty levels. You'll find a good mix, with about 10 easy routes, 55 moderate routes, and 29 difficult routes. This variety ensures options for different abilities, from beginners to experienced riders.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Baza?
A: Yes, Baza offers several family-friendly mountain bike trails, particularly among the 10 easy routes available. These trails are generally shorter and have less elevation gain, making them suitable for families looking for a more relaxed ride through the natural park's diverse landscapes.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Baza?
A: The Sierra de Baza Natural Park is generally welcoming to dogs, but it's always best to check specific trail regulations. When riding with your dog, ensure they are well-behaved, kept under control, and that you clean up after them to preserve the tranquil environment for everyone.

Q: What kind of terrain and natural features can I expect on the trails?
A: The trails in Baza, especially within the Sierra de Baza Natural Park, offer diverse terrain. You'll ride through captivating pine forests, including black and Scots pines, ancient poplars, and willows. The geological formations contribute to unique landscapes, providing varied scenery and natural shelter throughout your ride.

Q: What are some scenic viewpoints or landmarks to look out for?
A: The Sierra de Baza Natural Park is rich in scenic spots. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3251692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Narváez Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for stunning vistas. Other notable natural features include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2551692" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Prados del Rey&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3251662" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fountain of Health&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes traverse different villages and hamlets, offering cultural insights alongside natural beauty.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available?
A: Yes, many mountain bike routes around Baza are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1144884904/eye-of-the-heredad-circular-by-zujar-from-baza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Eye of the Heredad - circular by Zujar from Baza&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular path, and the more challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1013417223/morron-alto-desde-baza-circular-por-el-parque-natural-sierra-de-baza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Morrón Alto desde Baza — circular por el Parque Natural Sierra de Baza&lt;/a&gt; also offers a circular experience.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Baza?
A: Baza offers a pleasant environment for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide the most comfortable temperatures for riding. While the region is known for its tranquility, checking the local weather forecast before heading out is always advisable.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Baza?
A: The mountain bike trails in Baza are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 90 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the extensive network of trails within the Sierra de Baza Natural Park, and the serene, natural environment that allows for a peaceful cycling experience.

Q: Are there places to stay or eat near the mountain bike trails?
A: Yes, Baza and the surrounding area offer various amenities for cyclists. You can find accommodation options, including hostels like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7119164" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorillo Hostel&lt;/a&gt;, and campsites such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Fraguara Campsite&lt;/a&gt;. There are also huts and refuges like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4487636" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Canaleja Alta Refuge&lt;/a&gt;. Baza itself has cafes and pubs, and you can find bike rental and repair services at places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7340918" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bike Cueva Bike Rental & Repair Shop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How long are the mountain bike trails around Baza?
A: The length of mountain bike trails around Baza varies significantly. You can find shorter, easier routes, while more challenging trails can extend considerably. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1145095623/puerto-de-los-tejos-a-mirador-de-barea-desde-la-casa-forestal-floranes-circular-por-parque-natural-sierra-de-baza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de los Tejos a Mirador de Barea desde la Casa Forestal Floranes&lt;/a&gt; route is about 31 miles (50 km) long, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1141745847/los-prados-del-rey-desde-baza-circular-por-parque-natural-sierra-de-baza" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Prados del Rey desde Baza&lt;/a&gt; trail is approximately 34 miles (55 km).

Q: Is there parking available at the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not listed, the Sierra de Baza Natural Park offers various public use facilities, and many routes are accessible from Baza itself. It is generally possible to find parking near popular access points or in the villages surrounding the park. Detailed cycling guides for the park often provide information on access and parking.
tips: Coming from Baza, this junction gives you the option of continuing towards the Canaleja Alta Refuge or turning right and exploring other trails. If you continue straight you will pass by two long and low buildings. There are lovely views from this area.
It is an incredible place full of pine trees. The surface of this place is waterproof, so when the snow melts, the famous wet meadows are formed, forming a postcard panorama.

The path skirts the slopes of the Fonfría ravine and when it begins to descend it enters a repopulated pine forest where the route disappears, there are stone milestones but they are not enough, it is important to have an idea of basic orientation to stay on course and follow the track, the Pino de la Señora is "hidden" in the forest and does not appear by chance, you have to get to the clearing where the magnificent specimen is, considered the oldest and most monumental tree in the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. The pine is leaning to one side because for a time it was used to extract resin and the branches grew towards the side where the cut was not, in this way the weight of the branches has brought the old pine to the edge of collapse.
Baza is a city whose municipal area is the largest in the province of Granada, whose population centers depend on it: Baúl (where the section of the greenway begins), La Jamula and Benacebada.

faqs.question: Is this mountain bike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this is classified as a "Hard mountain bike ride". It demands "very good fitness" and "advanced riding skills." Be prepared for steep ascents and descents, and uneven trails; some sections may even require you to dismount and push your bike.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied. You'll ride through small forest paths, wider forest trails, and zigzagging sections through beautiful pine forests. The route flattens out as it reaches the high mountain meadows of Prados del Rey, characterized by a unique "lagoon land" topography. Expect steep ascents and descents, and uneven trails with loose features, especially in areas with historical mining activity and limestone "calares."

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll experience dense pine forests, including an endemic Scots pine subspecies, and expansive high mountain meadows like Prados del Rey, which are considered a magical place. Keep an eye out for ancient, enormous pine trees such as La Señora Pine. The trail also passes through areas with historical mining remnants and offers panoramic views, potentially including the snow-capped peaks of the Sierra Nevada in the distance.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de Baza Natural Park offers different experiences throughout the year. Spring is particularly beautiful when the high mountain meadows transform into a "multicolor floral tapestry." However, given the high altitude and demanding nature of the trail, it's crucial to check weather conditions, especially for snowmelt in spring or potential winter conditions. Summer can be very hot, so early mornings are advisable.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Los Prados del Rey desde Baza trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Baza, which offers various parking options within the town. For specific trailhead parking, it's best to consult a detailed map or the Komoot app for the most convenient starting points near the Historic Center of Baza or the outskirts leading into the park.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally allowed within the Sierra de Baza Natural Park. However, it is always recommended to keep them on a leash, especially to protect local wildlife and ensure their safety on challenging terrain. Be mindful of other trail users and any specific park regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for the Sierra de Baza Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no entrance fees to access the Sierra de Baza Natural Park for recreational activities like mountain biking. However, specific activities or large groups might require permits. It's always a good idea to check the official website of the Natural Park of Sierra de Baza for the most up-to-date regulations regarding permits, especially if you plan any organized events or activities beyond typical recreational use.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths within the park. You'll find sections that overlap with trails such as Barranco de la Fonfría y Pino de la Señora, Andalucía - South Route (Provincia de Granada), and the Cañada del Camino Real de Lorca, among others. These intersections can offer opportunities for extending or modifying your ride if you're familiar with the area.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging mountain bike ride?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and length (55 km), you should bring plenty of water, high-energy snacks, a repair kit for your bike (including spare tubes), a first-aid kit, and appropriate clothing layers for varying mountain weather. A GPS device or the Komoot app with the downloaded route is essential for navigation, as some sections might be remote.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities like cafes or restaurants near the trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends near Baza, where you'll find a full range of amenities including cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options. However, once you enter the Sierra de Baza Natural Park, especially in the higher mountain areas like Prados del Rey, amenities are scarce to non-existent. Plan to be self-sufficient for the duration of your ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This circular route is approximately 55 km long with over 1,280 meters of elevation gain. Komoot estimates the duration at around 5 hours and 10 minutes of pure riding time. However, due to its 'difficult' rating and the need for advanced skills, allow for extra time for breaks, enjoying the scenery, and potentially pushing your bike on challenging sections. A full day should be allocated for this adventure.
